evals.prompt: Plan a sprint from SPEC-0015 for the claude-plugin-sdd repo. Use GitHub issues. The repo is joestump/claude-plugin-sdd.
evals.expected_output: Creates an epic issue and 3-4 story issues in GitHub with requirement checklists, branch naming, PR conventions, foundation story detection, hotspot analysis, and a dependency graph.

assertions.text: Epic issue created with 'epic' label and title starting with 'Implement'
3-4 story issues created, each with a ## Requirements section containing task checklists
Each requirement checklist item references SPEC-0015 and includes WHEN/THEN scenarios derived from the spec
Each story issue body has a ### Branch section with feature/{issue-number}-{slug} pattern after issue creation (two-pass)
Each story issue body has a ### PR Convention section with 'Closes #{issue-number}' keyword
Branch slug is kebab-case derived from the story title, max 50 chars
Foundation story detection ran: shared types/packages needed by 2+ stories identified and extracted into foundation-labeled stories
Hotspot analysis ran: recent git history analyzed and hotspot files reported with percentages
Dependency graph output showing foundation stories, serialized stories (if hotspots), and parallelizable stories
Every ### Requirement section from the spec appears in exactly one story's task checklist
GitHub Project V2 created and linked to the repository
Report suggests /sdd:prime before starting implementation

evals.prompt: Break down SPEC-0016 into GitHub issues for joestump/claude-plugin-sdd. Use --no-branches and --no-projects flags.
evals.expected_output: Creates epic and story issues without ### Branch, ### PR Convention sections, and without creating a GitHub Project.

assertions.text: Epic issue created with 'epic' label
3-4 story issues created with ## Requirements sections
No ### Branch section appears in any issue body
No ### PR Convention section appears in any issue body
No GitHub Project created (report says 'skipped')
Report confirms branch naming was skipped and PR conventions were skipped
Stories that involve HTTP endpoints include a ## Security Checklist section with authentication, input validation, output encoding, rate limiting, and body size limits
Security checklist is NOT added to stories that don't involve HTTP endpoints
CI foundation story created with static analysis, race detection, and formatting checks (language-agnostic terms)
CI story uses 'ci' and 'foundation' labels

evals.prompt: Plan a sprint from SPEC-0012 with --scrum flag. Use GitHub issues for joestump/claude-plugin-sdd.
evals.expected_output: Runs full scrum ceremony: spec completeness audit, issue decomposition, 5-agent grooming team, dissent resolution, automatic organize, automatic enrich, and sprint report.

assertions.text: User informed that scrum ceremony is starting with expected phases
Spec completeness audit ran: checked for design.md pairing and reported pass/missing counts
Five specialist agents spawned: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Engineer A, Engineer B (Grumpy), Architect
Each agent reviewed every story and submitted a verdict (APPROVED, REVISE, or DEFER)
Dissent resolution ran for any stories with non-APPROVED verdicts
Organize step ran automatically after grooming (no user prompt required)
Enrich step ran automatically after organize (no user prompt required)
Sprint report output contains: Spec Completeness, Grooming Results (Accepted/Revised/Deferred), Final Sprint Backlog, and Next Steps sections
Sprint report includes story sizes (XS/S/M/L/XL) assigned by Scrum Master
Sprint report recommends /sdd:work and /sdd:prime as next steps

evals.prompt: Plan a sprint from SPEC-9999 for joestump/claude-plugin-sdd using GitHub issues.
evals.expected_output: Reports that the spec cannot be found and suggests creating one with /sdd:spec.

assertions.text: Error message indicates SPEC-9999 was not found or does not exist
Suggests /sdd:spec to create a new specification
No issues created in the tracker
No GitHub Project created

evals.prompt: Work on SPEC-0015 issues for joestump/claude-plugin-sdd. Use --max-agents 2.
evals.expected_output: Discovers open issues for SPEC-0015, creates worktrees, dispatches up to 2 parallel workers with sibling PR manifest, implements issues, creates PRs with lifecycle label transitions.

assertions.text: Parallelism limit resolved to 2 from --max-agents flag
Report shows 'Starting N of M ready stories (Q queued, max-parallel-agents: 2)'
Lifecycle labels (queued, in-progress, in-review, merged) created or verified to exist
All workable issues initially labeled 'queued'
Issues transitioned from 'queued' to 'in-progress' when assigned to a worker (previous label removed first)
Issues transitioned from 'in-progress' to 'in-review' when PR created (previous label removed first)
Sibling PR manifest built before dispatching workers, containing file ownership and shared types sections
Each worker received spec.md, design.md, ADR content, and sibling PR manifest in their context
Workers broadcast FILE_CLAIM via SendMessage before modifying files
No more than 2 agents active concurrently; excess stories queued
Queue status reported after each agent completion: 'Active: N/2, Queued: Q, Completed: C, Failed: F, Blocked: B'
Final report includes Results table with Issue, Branch, PR, and Status columns

evals.prompt: Run /sdd:work --dry-run for SPEC-0010 in joestump/claude-plugin-sdd.
evals.expected_output: Outputs a preview table of what would happen without creating worktrees, branches, or PRs.

assertions.text: Output contains '## Dry Run' heading
Preview table includes columns: #, Issue, Branch, Status
Blocked issues shown with dependency reason (e.g., 'Blocked (depends on #XX)')
Skipped issues listed with reasons (epic, no ### Branch section)
Output ends with 'No changes were made.'
No git worktrees created
No branches created
No PRs created
No lifecycle labels applied to issues

evals.prompt: Implement issues 42 43 44 for joestump/claude-plugin-sdd. The CLAUDE.md has max-parallel-agents set to 3.
evals.expected_output: Picks up the 3 specific issues, creates worktrees, dispatches workers respecting the CLAUDE.md parallelism config, validates protected paths, and creates PRs.

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md read for ## SDD Configuration section to resolve max-parallel-agents
Worktrees created at .claude/worktrees/{branch-name} for each issue
Workers validate diff against protected paths (spec-dir, adr-dir, CLAUDE.md, .claude-plugin-design.json) before staging
If any protected file was modified, it is reverted via git checkout and recorded in ### Deferred Design Doc Updates PR body section
Governing comments added in source code files (not subject to design doc isolation)
Each PR body includes close keywords referencing the issue number
Topological merge order computed after all PRs reach in-review, showing tier classification
Merge order table shown to user with Order, PR, Issue, Overlapping Files, and Action columns
User asked to approve merge order before merging begins

evals.prompt: Run /sdd:work with no arguments for joestump/claude-plugin-sdd.
evals.expected_output: Reviews the full backlog, proposes a batch of issues biased toward unblocking work, and asks the user to approve before starting.

assertions.text: Backlog scanned: all open non-epic issues fetched from the tracker
Issues without ### Branch sections skipped with suggestion to run /sdd:enrich
Proposed batch presented via AskUserQuestion with a table showing Issue, Project/Epic, and Rationale columns
Batch proposal biases toward issues that unblock other issues
User given options: 'Approve this batch' or 'Let me pick manually'
No work started until user approves the batch
Dependency ordering respected: blocked issues placed in deferred queue

evals.prompt: Review PRs for SPEC-0009 in joestump/claude-plugin-sdd. Use 2 reviewer-responder pairs.
evals.expected_output: Discovers open PRs for SPEC-0009, runs conflict-marker CI gate, creates reviewer-responder pairs, reviews against spec acceptance criteria, and merges approved PRs.

assertions.text: Conflict-marker CI gate ran on ALL target PR diffs before any review logic
PR diffs scanned for <<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>> markers
spec.md and design.md loaded as architecture context for reviewers
Team created with 2 reviewer agents and 2 responder agents
PRs distributed to pairs using round-robin assignment
Reviewers checked CI status before reviewing each PR diff
Reviews reference specific spec acceptance criteria or ADR decisions, not just style
Approved PRs merged using squash strategy (default)
Parent epic closure checked after each successful merge
Final report contains Results table with PR, Title, CI, Review, Merge, and Status columns
Final report includes Worktrees section showing reused/created/cleaned counts

evals.prompt: Review PRs 101 102 103 for joestump/claude-plugin-sdd with --dry-run.
evals.expected_output: Outputs a preview table showing which PRs would be reviewed and by which pair, without submitting any reviews or merging.

assertions.text: Output contains '## Dry Run' heading
Preview table includes columns: #, PR, Title, Branch, CI, Pair
Each PR assigned to a pair via round-robin
Output ends with 'No reviews submitted. No merges performed.'
No review comments submitted to any PR
No PRs merged
No team created (dry-run exits before team formation)

evals.prompt: Review PRs for SPEC-0015 in joestump/claude-plugin-sdd with --no-merge. Assume PR #201 has conflict markers in its diff.
evals.expected_output: PR #201 rejected immediately by the conflict-marker CI gate with REQUEST_CHANGES. Other PRs reviewed normally but not merged due to --no-merge.

assertions.text: Conflict-marker gate ran before any review logic for all PRs
PR #201 received REQUEST_CHANGES review with '## Conflict Markers Detected' heading
Rejection message for #201 includes a table of offending file paths and line numbers
PR #201 skipped entirely for architecture context loading and spec compliance review
PR #201 reported as 'Rejected: conflict markers' in the final summary
Other PRs reviewed normally against spec acceptance criteria
No PRs merged (--no-merge flag respected)
Approved PRs reported as 'approved, pending manual merge'

evals.prompt: Run a full project audit on joestump/claude-plugin-sdd with no scope filter.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive audit across all six drift categories with structured report, severity assignments, and recommended actions.

assertions.text: All six drift categories analyzed: Code vs. Spec, Code vs. ADR, ADR vs. Spec, Coverage Gaps, Stale Artifacts, Policy Violations
Report header shows scope as 'Full project' with counts of ADRs, specs, and source files analyzed
Spec pairing validation ran: each spec directory checked for both spec.md and design.md
Unpaired spec artifacts reported as [WARNING] under Stale Artifacts
MUST/SHALL violations assigned [CRITICAL] severity
SHOULD/RECOMMENDED violations assigned [WARNING] severity
Coverage gaps assigned [INFO] severity
Summary matrix table includes all six categories with Critical, Warning, Info, and Total columns
Recommended Actions list ordered by severity: critical first, then warning, then info
Findings within each category ordered by severity (critical first)
Full artifact identifiers used (ADR-XXXX, SPEC-XXXX), not abbreviated
File paths include line numbers where possible (e.g., src/path/file.ts:45)

evals.prompt: Audit the architecture of joestump/claude-plugin-sdd with --scrum flag. Focus on the security topic.
evals.expected_output: Runs standard audit analysis scoped to security, then enters scrum triage ceremony with 5-agent team for prioritization and dispute resolution.

assertions.text: Standard audit analysis completed first (steps 4-6) before triage ceremony begins
Audit scoped to security-related artifacts and code
Five triage agents spawned: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Engineer A, Engineer B (Grumpy), Architect
Findings grouped by functional theme, not by drift category
Engineer B provides substantive challenges to findings, not generic approvals
ADRs (accepted) and specs (approved/implemented) treated as source of truth
Triage report produced with remediation priorities
--review mode NOT activated (--scrum takes precedence)

evals.prompt: Run /sdd:audit on an empty project directory that has no docs/adrs/ or docs/openspec/specs/ directories.
evals.expected_output: Reports that no design artifacts were found and suggests creating ADRs or specs.

assertions.text: Message indicates docs/adrs/ directory does not exist, suggests /sdd:adr
Message indicates docs/openspec/specs/ directory does not exist, suggests /sdd:spec
Reports 'No design artifacts found' when neither ADRs nor specs exist
No audit report table produced (nothing to analyze)
Does not error or crash on missing directories

evals.prompt: Review PRs for SPEC-0003 in joestump/claude-plugin-sdd. No open PRs exist for this spec.
evals.expected_output: Informs the user that no open PRs were found and suggests running /sdd:work to create them.

assertions.text: Message indicates no open PRs were found matching SPEC-0003
Suggests running /sdd:work to create PRs from planned issues
No team created (nothing to review)
No reviews submitted

evals.prompt: Create a spec for our new webhook-delivery service. It will expose REST endpoints for registering webhook URLs, listing subscriptions, and triggering test deliveries. The service reads events from a Kafka topic and fans out HTTP POST requests to registered endpoints with retries. We're building this in Go with a PostgreSQL database for subscription storage.
evals.expected_output: Creates paired spec.md and design.md under docs/openspec/specs/webhook-delivery/. The spec includes a Security Requirements section (web-facing: REST endpoints over HTTP), auth-by-default endpoint table with all endpoints defaulting to Auth: Required, and backend quality guidelines for error handling (service boundaries, external HTTP calls), concurrency (fan-out workers, Kafka consumer), and database operations (PostgreSQL). No Accessibility Requirements section (API-only, no UI).

assertions.text: spec.md created at docs/openspec/specs/webhook-delivery/spec.md
design.md created at docs/openspec/specs/webhook-delivery/design.md (paired artifact)
spec.md contains a '## Security Requirements' section with all six subsections (Authentication, Rate Limiting, Security Headers, Request Body Size Limits, CSRF Protection, Redirect Validation)
Endpoint table uses auth-by-default: all endpoints show 'Auth: Required' unless explicitly marked 'Auth: Public' with justification
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Error Handling Standards' with error wrapping, sentinel errors, no silent swallowing, and structured logging
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Concurrency Safety' with context propagation, worker lifecycle, race safety, and race detection CI
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Database Operation Standards' with transactions, connection lifecycle, and parameterized queries
spec.md does NOT contain an '## Accessibility Requirements' section (API-only, no UI)
spec.md uses RFC 2119 keywords (MUST, SHALL, SHOULD, MAY, etc.) for all normative requirements
spec.md uses SPEC-XXXX numbering (not RFC-XXXX)
design.md contains at least one Mermaid diagram
Backend quality guidelines use language-agnostic terms (not Go-specific like 'goroutines' or 'slog')

evals.prompt: We need a spec for an admin dashboard. It's a web UI built with Go html/template and HTMX that lets ops staff manage feature flags, view system health, and browse audit logs. It has server-rendered pages with modals for creating/editing flags, a real-time status panel that auto-refreshes, and REST endpoints backing the UI. The backend uses PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: Creates paired spec.md and design.md. The spec is both web-facing (REST endpoints, HTTP server) AND UI-facing (html/template, HTMX, modals, dashboard). It includes BOTH Security Requirements and Accessibility Requirements sections, plus auth-by-default endpoint tables and backend quality guidelines for error handling and database operations.

assertions.text: spec.md created at docs/openspec/specs/admin-dashboard/spec.md
design.md created at docs/openspec/specs/admin-dashboard/design.md (paired artifact)
spec.md contains a '## Security Requirements' section (web-facing: REST endpoints)
spec.md contains an '## Accessibility Requirements' section (UI-facing: html/template, HTMX, modals)
Accessibility section includes WCAG 2.1 AA, ARIA landmarks, aria-label for icon-only controls, aria-live for dynamic content (auto-refresh panel), keyboard navigation, and focus management (modals)
Endpoint table uses auth-by-default: all endpoints default to 'Auth: Required'
spec.md contains backend quality requirements for error handling and database operations
spec.md uses RFC 2119 keywords for all normative requirements
Scenarios use exactly #### heading level (not ### or bullets)
Every requirement has at least one scenario with WHEN/THEN format

evals.prompt: Create a spec for a CLI tool called db-migrate that runs database schema migrations. It reads migration files from a directory, connects to PostgreSQL, applies them in order inside transactions, and logs results. It's a command-line tool only -- no web server, no UI, no HTTP endpoints. It does handle concurrent migration locking to prevent two instances from running at once.
evals.expected_output: Creates paired spec.md and design.md. This is a CLI tool with NO web or UI component. It MUST NOT include Security Requirements or Accessibility Requirements sections. It SHOULD include backend quality guidelines for error handling (database operations, file I/O), concurrency (migration locking), and database operations (transactions, connections, parameterized queries).

assertions.text: spec.md created at docs/openspec/specs/db-migrate/spec.md
design.md created at docs/openspec/specs/db-migrate/design.md (paired artifact)
spec.md does NOT contain a '## Security Requirements' section (CLI tool, not web-facing)
spec.md does NOT contain an '## Accessibility Requirements' section (CLI tool, not UI-facing)
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Error Handling Standards' (database operations, file I/O can fail)
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Concurrency Safety' (concurrent migration locking)
spec.md contains '### Requirement: Database Operation Standards' (transactions, connections, parameterized queries)
spec.md uses SPEC-XXXX numbering (not RFC-XXXX)
design.md contains at least one Mermaid diagram

evals.prompt: Check src/api/ for drift against our ADRs and specs
evals.expected_output: Performs a drift check on the src/api/ directory. Scans for security lint patterns (unbounded body read, template safety bypass, user-controlled redirect, missing auth middleware, DOM injection, CDN without SRI) in source files. Produces a findings table with Severity, Category, Finding, Source, and Location columns. Security lint findings use category 'Security Lint' and severity [WARNING].

assertions.text: Output contains '## Drift Check: src/api/' heading
Output contains a findings table with columns: Severity, Category, Finding, Source, Location
Security lint findings (if any) use category 'Security Lint' and severity '[WARNING]'
Security lint scan checks for all 6 patterns: unbounded body read, template safety bypass, user-controlled redirect, missing auth middleware, DOM injection, CDN without SRI
Each security lint finding includes file path with line number in Location column
Each security lint finding includes a remediation suggestion
Output ends with a Summary section showing Critical/Warning/Info counts
Output includes suggested actions based on findings

evals.prompt: Run a drift check on our templates/ directory
evals.expected_output: Performs a drift check on the templates/ directory. Scans for template quality patterns: duplicate inline script blocks, duplicate form structures, duplicate navigation/sidebar markup, dev-only CDN URLs, and multiple JS interaction frameworks. Template quality findings use category 'Template Quality' with individually defined severities ([WARNING] for duplicate scripts/nav/dev CDN, [INFO] for duplicate forms/framework sprawl).

assertions.text: Output contains '## Drift Check: templates/' heading
Template quality scan checks for all 5 patterns: duplicate inline scripts, duplicate form structure, duplicate navigation/sidebar, dev-only CDN URLs, multiple JS frameworks
Template quality findings use category 'Template Quality'
Duplicate inline script findings use severity '[WARNING]'
Duplicate form structure findings use severity '[INFO]'
Dev-only CDN findings use severity '[WARNING]'
Multiple JS framework findings use severity '[INFO]'
Each template quality finding includes affected file paths in Location column

evals.prompt: Check this project for drift. There are no ADRs or specs yet.
evals.expected_output: Reports that no design artifacts were found and suggests creating them. Does NOT proceed with analysis. Suggests using /sdd:adr or /sdd:spec to create first artifacts.

assertions.text: Output reports 'No design artifacts found' (or equivalent message when neither ADR nor spec directories exist)
Output suggests '/sdd:adr' to create first ADR
Output suggests '/sdd:spec' to create first spec
Does NOT produce a findings table (no artifacts to check against)

evals.prompt: Discover what implicit architecture decisions exist in this codebase. We haven't documented anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Produces a discovery report analyzing the entire project. Spawns 4 parallel agents for: dependency/framework analysis, architectural pattern analysis, project structure/boundary analysis, and configuration/infrastructure analysis. Report includes suggested ADRs (implicit decisions) and suggested specs (subsystem boundaries), each with confidence levels and copy-paste-ready commands. Does NOT create any files.

assertions.text: Output contains '## Discovery Report' heading
Output contains a '### Suggested ADRs' section with a table including Confidence, Decision, Evidence, and Command columns
Output contains a '### Suggested Specs' section with a table including Confidence, Subsystem, Boundary, and Command columns
Every suggestion includes a confidence level (High, Medium, or Low)
Every ADR suggestion includes a copy-paste-ready '/sdd:adr' command in the Command column
Every spec suggestion includes a copy-paste-ready '/sdd:spec' command in the Command column
Suggestions are sorted by confidence (High first, then Medium, then Low) within each section
Every suggestion cites specific evidence from the codebase (file paths, dependency declarations, config entries)
Output contains a '### Next Steps' section
No files are created or modified (read-only skill)

evals.prompt: Discover architecture in the src/payments/ directory
evals.expected_output: If src/payments/ does not exist, reports the error and stops without falling back to full-project analysis. If it does exist, scopes the discovery to that directory only.

assertions.text: If scope path does not exist, output contains 'Scope not found: src/payments/' error message
If scope path does not exist, does NOT fall back to full-project analysis
If scope path exists, output contains '## Discovery Report' scoped to src/payments/
No files are created or modified (read-only skill)

evals.prompt: Generate a documentation site for our project. We have ADRs in docs/adrs/ and specs in docs/openspec/specs/.
evals.expected_output: Runs pre-flight checks (Node.js, ADR/spec existence), detects no existing Docusaurus site, proceeds with scaffold mode. Creates docs-site/ directory from templates/docusaurus/, customizes package.json and docusaurus.config.ts, runs npm install, creates .sdd-docs.json manifest with tracked files and SHA-256 checksums, and updates .claudeignore.

Pre-flight check verifies ADR and/or spec directories have content
docs-site/ directory created from templates/docusaurus/ via cp -r
docs-site/package.json customized with project name
docs-site/docusaurus.config.ts customized with project title and URLs

Offers to start the dev server after completion

Does NOT create a docs-site/ directory

files.content:
## Architecture Context

This project uses the [SDD plugin](https://github.com/joestump/claude-plugin-sdd) for architecture governance.

- Architecture Decision Records are in `docs/adrs/`
- Specifications are in `docs/openspec/specs/`

evals.prompt: /sdd:prime authentication
evals.expected_output: Loads architecture context filtered by 'authentication' topic, showing only semantically relevant ADRs and specs

assertions.text: Checks CLAUDE.md for SDD plugin references before scanning
Scans ADR and spec directories for artifacts
Applies semantic topic filter — matches ADRs/specs related to auth, sessions, tokens, access control
Output includes 'Matching ADRs' and 'Matching Specs' tables with Relevance column
Output includes 'Skipped' section listing non-matching artifacts
Output includes 'Summaries' section with 2-3 sentence summaries of matching artifacts
No files are modified (read-only skill)

files.content:
## Architecture Context

- Architecture Decision Records are in `docs/adrs/`
- Specifications are in `docs/openspec/specs/`

---
status: accepted
date: 2025-06-15
---

# ADR-0001: Use JWT for API Authentication

## Context and Problem Statement

We need a stateless authentication mechanism for our API.

## Decision Outcome

Chosen option: JWT tokens, because they enable stateless auth.

---
status: accepted
date: 2025-07-01
---

# ADR-0002: Use PostgreSQL for Primary Database

## Context and Problem Statement

We need a relational database for user data.

## Decision Outcome

Chosen option: PostgreSQL, because of its reliability.

Does NOT attempt to scan for ADRs or specs

files.content:
# My Project

This is a web application.

Output contains a Specifications table with ID, Title, Status, Date columns
Does NOT include an Architecture Decisions table
Specs are sorted by SPEC number
No files are modified (read-only skill)

files.content:
## Architecture Context

- Architecture Decision Records are in `docs/adrs/`
- Specifications are in `docs/openspec/specs/`

evals.prompt: /sdd:status ADR-0003 accepted
evals.expected_output: Updates ADR-0003 YAML frontmatter status from proposed to accepted

assertions.text: Locates the ADR-0003 file by globbing docs/adrs/ADR-0003-*.md
Edits only the status field in the YAML frontmatter
Status changed from 'proposed' to 'accepted'
No content outside YAML frontmatter is modified
Reports the change: 'proposed -> accepted'

files.content:
## Architecture Context

- Architecture Decision Records are in `docs/adrs/`
- Specifications are in `docs/openspec/specs/`

---
status: proposed
date: 2025-10-01
decision-makers: [team-lead]
---

# ADR-0003: Use Event-Driven Architecture

## Context and Problem Statement

We need to decouple services for scalability.

## Decision Outcome

Chosen option: Event-driven with message queues.

evals.prompt: /sdd:status
evals.expected_output: Lists available ADRs and specs with current statuses, then prompts user to choose which to update

assertions.text: Detects missing identifier argument
Scans for available ADRs and specs and presents them with current statuses
Uses AskUserQuestion to prompt user for which artifact to update
Shows valid status options (proposed/accepted/deprecated/superseded for ADRs, draft/review/approved/implemented/deprecated for specs)

# SPEC-0005: Notification Service

## Overview

Real-time notification delivery system.

### Requirement: Email Notifications

The system MUST support email delivery.

### Requirement: Push Notifications

The system SHOULD support push notifications.

# SPEC-0005: Notification Service — Design

## Architecture

Event-driven notification pipeline.

evals.prompt: Organize issues for SPEC-0003 --module worker
evals.expected_output: Resolves spec within the worker module's artifact paths and organizes issues scoped to that module

assertions.text: Artifact path resolution invoked with --module worker
Spec resolved from worker module's spec directory, not project root
Validates spec pairing (spec.md + design.md) within the module
Reads tracker config from CLAUDE.md via Config Resolution pattern
Presents three-tier intervention choice via AskUserQuestion
Uses ToolSearch to discover tracker project tools at runtime

evals.prompt: Add branch names and PR conventions to all SPEC-0005 issues
evals.expected_output: Enriches existing tracker issues for SPEC-0005 with ### Branch and ### PR Convention sections

assertions.text: Resolves SPEC-0005 by reading spec.md from the spec directory
Validates spec pairing (checks both spec.md and design.md exist)
Reads Branch Conventions and PR Conventions from CLAUDE.md ### SDD Configuration
Detects tracker via Config Resolution pattern
For each issue: checks if ### Branch section already exists before adding (idempotent)
For each issue: checks if ### PR Convention section already exists before adding (idempotent)
Branch slug derived from issue title in kebab-case, max 50 chars
Epic issues get epic/ prefix, task issues get feature/ prefix
Reports summary: issues enriched, skipped, and any failures

assertions.text: Parses --branch-prefix hotfix and --dry-run flags from arguments

Uses 'hotfix' as the branch prefix instead of default 'feature'
For each issue: shows what ### Branch and ### PR Convention sections WOULD be added
Shows exact content that would be appended to each issue body
Indicates which issues would be skipped (already have sections)
No tracker issues are modified in --dry-run mode
